# nautilus_bitmex - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_bitmex](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_bitmex Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_bitmex/lib.rs.html#16-74)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [BitMEX](https://bitmex.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-bitmex` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models and helper utilities that wrap the official **BitMEX API**.

The official BitMEX API reference can be found at [https://www.bitmex.com/app/apiOverview](https://www.bitmex.com/app/apiOverview)
. All public links inside this crate reference the official documentation.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/index.html#platform)
Platform
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/index.html#reexports)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use crate::common::consts::[BITMEX_VENUE](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/common/consts/static.BITMEX_VENUE.html "static nautilus_bitmex::common::consts::BITMEX_VENUE") ;`

`pub use crate::common::enums::[BitmexSide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/common/enums/enum.BitmexSide.html "enum nautilus_bitmex::common::enums::BitmexSide") ;`

`pub use crate::data::[BitmexDataClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/data/struct.BitmexDataClient.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::data::BitmexDataClient") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::[BitmexExecutionClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/struct.BitmexExecutionClient.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::execution::BitmexExecutionClient") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::canceller::[BroadcasterMetrics](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/canceller/struct.BroadcasterMetrics.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::execution::canceller::BroadcasterMetrics") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::canceller::[CancelBroadcaster](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/canceller/struct.CancelBroadcaster.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::execution::canceller::CancelBroadcaster") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::canceller::[CancelBroadcasterConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/canceller/struct.CancelBroadcasterConfig.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::execution::canceller::CancelBroadcasterConfig") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::canceller::[ClientStats](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/canceller/struct.ClientStats.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::execution::canceller::ClientStats") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::submitter::[SubmitBroadcaster](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/submitter/struct.SubmitBroadcaster.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::execution::submitter::SubmitBroadcaster") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::submitter::[SubmitBroadcasterConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/submitter/struct.SubmitBroadcasterConfig.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::execution::submitter::SubmitBroadcasterConfig") ;`

`pub use crate::http::client::[BitmexHttpClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/http/client/struct.BitmexHttpClient.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::http::client::BitmexHttpClient") ;`

`pub use crate::http::error::[BitmexHttpError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/http/error/enum.BitmexHttpError.html "enum nautilus_bitmex::http::error::BitmexHttpError") ;`

`pub use crate::websocket::client::[BitmexWebSocketClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/websocket/client/struct.BitmexWebSocketClient.html "struct nautilus_bitmex::websocket::client::BitmexWebSocketClient") ;`

`pub use crate::websocket::error::[BitmexWsError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/websocket/error/enum.BitmexWsError.html "enum nautilus_bitmex::websocket::error::BitmexWsError") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/index.html#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/common/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::common")

Common types and utilities shared across the BitMEX adapter.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/config/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::config")

Configuration types for the BitMEX adapter clients.

[data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/data/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::data")

Live market data client implementation for the BitMEX adapter.

[error](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/error/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::error")

Unified error handling for the BitMEX adapter.

[execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/execution/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::execution")

Live execution client implementation for the BitMEX adapter.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/http/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::http")

HTTP REST API client implementation for BitMEX.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/python/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::python")

Python bindings from `pyo3`.

[websocket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_bitmex/websocket/index.html "mod nautilus_bitmex::websocket")

WebSocket client implementation for BitMEX real-time data feeds.

# Concepts | NautilusTrader Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: nightly

On this page

Concept guides introduce and explain the foundational ideas, components, and best practices that underpin the NautilusTrader platform. These guides are designed to provide both conceptual and practical insights, helping you navigate the system's architecture, strategies, data management, execution flow, and more. Explore the following guides to deepen your understanding and make the most of NautilusTrader.

[Overview](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/overview)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#overview "Direct link to overview")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Overview** guide covers the main features and intended use cases for the platform.

[Architecture](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/architecture)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#architecture "Direct link to architecture")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Architecture** guide dives deep into the foundational principles, structures, and designs that underpin the platform. It is ideal for developers, system architects, or anyone curious about the inner workings of NautilusTrader.

[Actors](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/actors)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#actors "Direct link to actors")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `Actor` serves as the foundational component for interacting with the trading system. The **Actors** guide covers capabilities and implementation specifics.

[Strategies](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/strategies)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#strategies "Direct link to strategies")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `Strategy` is at the heart of the NautilusTrader user experience when writing and working with trading strategies. The **Strategies** guide covers how to implement strategies for the platform.

[Instruments](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/instruments)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#instruments "Direct link to instruments")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Instruments** guide covers the different instrument definition specifications for tradable assets and contracts.

[Value Types](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/value_types)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#value-types "Direct link to value-types")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Value Types** guide covers the immutable numeric types (`Price`, `Quantity`, `Money`) used throughout the platform, including their arithmetic behavior, precision handling, and type-specific constraints.

[Data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/data)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#data "Direct link to data")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The NautilusTrader platform defines a range of built-in data types crafted specifically to represent a trading domain. The **Data** guide covers working with both built-in and custom data.

[Execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/execution)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#execution "Direct link to execution")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NautilusTrader can handle trade execution and order management for multiple strategies and venues simultaneously (per instance). The **Execution** guide covers components involved in execution, as well as the flow of execution messages (commands and events).

[Orders](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/orders)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#orders "Direct link to orders")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Orders** guide provides more details about the available order types for the platform, along with the execution instructions supported for each. Advanced order types and emulated orders are also covered.

[Positions](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/positions)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#positions "Direct link to positions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Positions** guide explains how positions work in NautilusTrader, including their lifecycle, aggregation from order fills, profit and loss calculations, and the important concept of position snapshotting for netting OMS configurations.

[Cache](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/cache)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#cache "Direct link to cache")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `Cache` is a central in-memory data store for managing all trading-related data. The **Cache** guide covers capabilities and best practices of the cache.

[Message Bus](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/message_bus)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#message-bus "Direct link to message-bus")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `MessageBus` is the core communication system enabling decoupled messaging patterns between components, including point-to-point, publish/subscribe, and request/response. The **Message Bus** guide covers capabilities and best practices of the `MessageBus`.

[Portfolio](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/portfolio)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#portfolio "Direct link to portfolio")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `Portfolio` serves as the central hub for managing and tracking all positions across active strategies for the trading node or backtest. It consolidates position data from multiple instruments, providing a unified view of your holdings, risk exposure, and overall performance. Explore this section to understand how NautilusTrader aggregates and updates portfolio state to support effective trading and risk management.

[Reports](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/reports)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#reports "Direct link to reports")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Reports** guide covers the reporting capabilities in NautilusTrader, including execution reports, portfolio analysis reports, PnL accounting considerations, and how reports are used for backtest post-run analysis.

[Logging](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/logging)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#logging "Direct link to logging")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The platform provides logging for both backtesting and live trading using a high-performance logger implemented in Rust.

[Backtesting](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/backtesting)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#backtesting "Direct link to backtesting")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Backtesting with NautilusTrader is a methodical simulation process that replicates trading activities using a specific system implementation.

[Visualization](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/visualization)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#visualization "Direct link to visualization")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Visualization** guide covers the interactive tearsheet system for analyzing backtest results, including available charts, themes, customization options, and how to create custom visualizations using the extensible chart registry.

[Live Trading](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/live)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#live-trading "Direct link to live-trading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Live trading in NautilusTrader enables traders to deploy their backtested strategies in real-time without code changes. This transition aims to maintain consistency, though there are key differences between backtesting and live trading.

[Adapters](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/adapters)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/concepts/#adapters "Direct link to adapters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The NautilusTrader design allows for integrating data providers and/or trading venues through adapter implementations. The **Adapters** guide covers requirements and best practices for developing new integration adapters for the platform.

note

The [API Reference](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/)
 documentation should be considered the source of truth for the platform. If there are any discrepancies between concepts described here and the API Reference, then the API Reference should be considered the correct information. We are working to ensure that concepts stay up-to-date with the API Reference and will be introducing doc tests in the near future to help with this.

# Python API | NautilusTrader Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: nightly

On this page

Welcome to the Python API reference for NautilusTrader!

The API reference provides detailed technical documentation for the NautilusTrader framework, including its modules, classes, methods, and functions. The reference is automatically generated from the latest NautilusTrader source code using [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/)
.

Please note that there are separate references for different versions of NautilusTrader:

*   **Latest**: This API reference is built from the head of the `develop` branch and represents the latest stable release.
*   **Nightly**: This API reference is built from the head of the `nightly` branch and represents bleeding edge and experimental changes/features currently in development.

You can select the desired API reference from the **Versions** top left drop down menu.

Use the right navigation sidebar to explore the available modules and their contents. You can click on any item to view its detailed documentation, including parameter descriptions, and return value explanations.

Why Python?[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/#why-python "Direct link to Why Python?")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Python was originally created decades ago as a simple scripting language with a clean straight forward syntax. It has since evolved into a fully fledged general purpose object-oriented programming language. Based on the TIOBE index, Python is currently the most popular programming language in the world. Not only that, Python has become the _de facto lingua franca_ of data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence.

*   [Why Python?](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/#why-python)

[](https://twitter.com/NautilusTrader)

# nautilus_data - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_data Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_data/lib.rs.html#16-70)

Expand description

Data engine and market data processing for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-data` crate provides a comprehensive framework for handling market data ingestion, processing, and aggregation within the NautilusTrader ecosystem. This includes real-time data streaming, historical data management, and various aggregation methodologies:

*   High-performance data engine for orchestrating data operations.
*   Data client infrastructure for connecting to market data providers.
*   Bar aggregation machinery supporting tick, volume, value, and time-based aggregation.
*   Order book management and delta processing capabilities.
*   Subscription management and data request handling.
*   Configurable data routing and processing pipelines.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
     (auto-enables `streaming`).
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.
*   `streaming`: Enables `persistence` dependency for catalog-based data streaming.
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#reexports)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use client::[DataClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/client/trait.DataClient.html "trait nautilus_data::client::DataClient") ;`

`pub use client::[DataClientAdapter](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/client/struct.DataClientAdapter.html "struct nautilus_data::client::DataClientAdapter") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[aggregation](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/aggregation/index.html "mod nautilus_data::aggregation")

Bar aggregation machinery.

[client](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/client/index.html "mod nautilus_data::client")

Base data client functionality.

[defi](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/defi/index.html "mod nautilus_data::defi")

DeFi (Decentralized Finance) integration for the data crate.

[engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_data/engine/index.html "mod nautilus_data::engine")

Provides a high-performance `DataEngine` for all environments.

---

# nautilus_cryptography - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_cryptography](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_cryptography Copy item path
===========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_cryptography/lib.rs.html#16-62)

Expand description

Cryptographic utilities and security functions for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-cryptography` crate provides essential cryptographic primitives and security utilities required for secure communication with trading venues and data providers. This includes digital signing, TLS configuration, and cryptographic provider management:

*   HMAC-based message authentication and signing.
*   Digital signatures using RSA and Ed25519 algorithms.
*   TLS client configuration with platform certificate verification.
*   Cryptographic provider management and initialization.
*   Secure encoding and decoding utilities.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#platform)
Platform
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

# nautilus_deribit - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_deribit](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_deribit Copy item path
======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_deribit/lib.rs.html#16-63)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [Deribit](https://www.deribit.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-deribit` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models and helper utilities that wrap the official **Deribit API v2**.

Deribit uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over both HTTP and WebSocket transports (not REST). WebSocket is preferred for subscriptions and real-time data.

The official Deribit API reference can be found at [https://docs.deribit.com/v2/](https://docs.deribit.com/v2/)
.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html#platform)
Platform
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/common/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::common")

Common types and utilities for the Deribit adapter.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/config/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::config")

Configuration structures for the Deribit adapter.

[data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/data/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::data")

Live market data client implementation for the Deribit adapter.

[execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/execution/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::execution")

Live execution client implementation for the Deribit adapter.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::factories")

Factory functions for creating Deribit clients and components.

Deribit HTTP client implementation using JSON-RPC 2.0.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/python/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::python")

Python bindings from `pyo3`.

[websocket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_deribit/websocket/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::websocket")

Deribit WebSocket client implementation using JSON-RPC 2.0.

# nautilus_common - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_common Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_common/lib.rs.html#16-88)

Expand description

Common componentry for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-common` crate provides shared components and utilities that form the system foundation for NautilusTrader applications. This includes the actor system, message bus, caching layer, and other essential services.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#platform)
Platform
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `stubs`: Enables type stubs for use in testing scenarios.
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support.
*   `indicators`: Includes the `nautilus-indicators` crate and indicator utilities.
*   `capnp`: Enables [Cap’n Proto](https://capnproto.org/)
     serialization support.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[actor](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/actor/index.html "mod nautilus_common::actor")

Actor system for event-driven message processing.

In-memory cache for market and execution data, with optional persistent backing.

Real-time and static `Clock` implementations.

Component system for managing stateful system entities.

DeFi (Decentralized Finance) integration for NautilusTrader.

[enums](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/enums/index.html "mod nautilus_common::enums")

Enumerations for common components.

Factories for constructing domain objects such as orders.

C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
.

Provides generation of identifiers such as `ClientOrderId` and `PositionId`.

Live (async/tokio) components for real-time trading.

The logging framework for Nautilus systems.

Message types for system communication.

[msgbus](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/msgbus/index.html "mod nautilus_common::msgbus")

A common in-memory `MessageBus` supporting multiple messaging patterns:

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_common/python/index.html "mod nautilus_common::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

# nautilus_databento - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_databento](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_databento Copy item path
========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_databento/lib.rs.html#16-67)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for [Databento](https://databento.com/)
.

The `nautilus-databento` crate provides a complete integration with the Databento API for accessing institutional-grade market data feeds across multiple venues and asset classes.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html#platform)
Platform
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case:

*   `live` (default): Enables live data functionality including the `data`, `factories`, and `live` modules.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html#modules)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Provides a unified data client that combines Databento’s live streaming and historical data capabilities.

[decode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/decode/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::decode")

Databento message decoding functions.

[enums](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/enums/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::enums")

Enumerations for the Databento integration.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::factories")

Factory functions for creating Databento clients and components.

[historical](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/historical/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::historical")

Core Databento historical client for both Rust and Python usage.

[live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/live/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::live")

[loader](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_databento/loader/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::loader")

.

| Main documentation | [https://docs.dydx.xyz](https://docs.dydx.xyz/) |
| Indexer HTTP API | [https://docs.dydx.exchange/api\_integration-indexer/indexer\_api](https://docs.dydx.exchange/api_integration-indexer/indexer_api) |
| Indexer WebSocket API | [https://docs.dydx.trade/developers/indexer/websockets](https://docs.dydx.trade/developers/indexer/websockets) |
| Order types | [https://docs.dydx.xyz/concepts/trading/orders](https://docs.dydx.xyz/concepts/trading/orders) |
| Permissioned keys | [https://docs.dydx.xyz/concepts/trading/authenticators](https://docs.dydx.xyz/concepts/trading/authenticators) |
| Validator client (gRPC) | [https://docs.dydx.exchange/api\_integration-clients/validator\_client](https://docs.dydx.exchange/api_integration-clients/validator_client) |

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_dydx/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_dydx/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_dydx/index.html#reexports)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# nautilus_core - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_core/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_core Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_core/lib.rs.html#16-106)

Expand description

Core foundational types and utilities for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-core` crate is designed to be lightweight, efficient, and to provide zero-cost abstractions wherever possible. It supplies the essential building blocks used across the NautilusTrader ecosystem, including:

*   Time handling and atomic clock functionality.
*   UUID generation and management.
*   Mathematical functions and interpolation utilities.
*   Correctness validation functions.
*   Serialization traits and helpers.
*   Cross-platform environment utilities.
*   Abstractions over common collections.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_core/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_core/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_core/index.html#reexports)

# nautilus_execution - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_execution Copy item path
========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_execution/lib.rs.html#16-76)

Expand description

Order execution system for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-execution` crate provides a comprehensive order execution system that handles the complete order lifecycle from submission to fill processing. This includes sophisticated order matching, execution venue integration, and advanced order type emulation:

*   **Execution engine**: Central orchestration of order routing and position management.
*   **Order matching engine**: High-fidelity market simulation for backtesting and paper trading.
*   **Order emulator**: Advanced order types not natively supported by venues (trailing stops, contingent orders).
*   **Execution clients**: Abstract interfaces for connecting to trading venues and brokers.
*   **Order manager**: Local order lifecycle management and state tracking.
*   **Matching core**: Low-level order book and price-time priority matching algorithms.
*   **Fee and fill models**: Configurable execution cost simulation and realistic fill behavior.

The crate supports both live trading environments (with real execution clients) and simulated environments (with matching engines), making it suitable for production trading, strategy development, and comprehensive backtesting.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#platform)
Platform
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#feature-flags)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

# nautilus_infrastructure - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_infrastructure](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_infrastructure Copy item path
=============================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_infrastructure/lib.rs.html#16-69)

Expand description

Database and messaging infrastructure for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-infrastructure` crate provides backend database implementations and message bus adapters that enable NautilusTrader to scale from development to production deployments. This includes enterprise-grade data persistence and messaging capabilities:

*   **Redis integration**: Cache database and message bus implementations using Redis.
*   **PostgreSQL integration**: SQL-based cache database with comprehensive data models.
*   **Connection management**: Robust connection handling with retry logic and health monitoring.
*   **Serialization options**: Support for JSON and MessagePack encoding formats.
*   **Python bindings**: PyO3 integration for seamless Python interoperability.

The crate supports multiple database backends through feature flags, allowing users to choose the appropriate infrastructure components for their specific deployment requirements and scale.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `redis`: Enables the Redis cache database and message bus backing implementations.
*   `postgres`: Enables the PostgreSQL SQLx models and cache database backend.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#modules)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/python/index.html "mod nautilus_infrastructure::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[redis](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/redis/index.html "mod nautilus_infrastructure::redis")

Provides a Redis backed `CacheDatabase` and `MessageBusDatabase` implementation.

[sql](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/sql/index.html "mod nautilus_infrastructure::sql")

SQL database implementations and utilities.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_hyperliquid/lib.rs.html#16-66)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [Hyperliquid](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs)
 decentralized exchange.

The `nautilus-hyperliquid` crate provides integration with the Hyperliquid API for trading perpetual futures on a decentralized exchange.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/index.html#reexports)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use crate::config::[HyperliquidDataClientConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidDataClientConfig.html "struct nautilus_hyperliquid::config::HyperliquidDataClientConfig") ;`

`pub use crate::config::[HyperliquidExecClientConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidExecClientConfig.html "struct nautilus_hyperliquid::config::HyperliquidExecClientConfig") ;`

`pub use crate::data::[HyperliquidDataClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/data/struct.HyperliquidDataClient.html "struct nautilus_hyperliquid::data::HyperliquidDataClient") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::[HyperliquidExecutionClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/execution/struct.HyperliquidExecutionClient.html "struct nautilus_hyperliquid::execution::HyperliquidExecutionClient") ;`

`pub use crate::http::client::[HyperliquidHttpClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/http/client/struct.HyperliquidHttpClient.html "struct nautilus_hyperliquid::http::client::HyperliquidHttpClient") ;`

`pub use crate::websocket::client::[HyperliquidWebSocketClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/websocket/client/struct.HyperliquidWebSocketClient.html "struct nautilus_hyperliquid::websocket::client::HyperliquidWebSocketClient") ;`

# nautilus_indicators - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_indicators](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_indicators Copy item path
=========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_indicators/lib.rs.html#16-74)

Expand description

Technical analysis indicators for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-indicators` crate provides a comprehensive collection of technical analysis indicators for quantitative trading and market research. This includes a wide variety of indicators organized by category, with a unified trait-based architecture for consistent usage:

*   **Moving averages**: SMA, EMA, DEMA, HMA, WMA, VWAP, adaptive averages, and linear regression.
*   **Momentum indicators**: RSI, MACD, Aroon, Bollinger Bands, CCI, Stochastics, and rate of change.
*   **Volatility indicators**: ATR, Donchian Channels, Keltner Channels, and volatility ratios.
*   **Ratio analysis**: Efficiency ratios and spread analysis for relative performance.
*   **Order book indicators**: Book imbalance ratio for analyzing market microstructure.
*   **Common indicator trait**: Unified interface supporting bars, quotes, trades, and order book data.

All indicators are designed for high-performance real-time processing with bounded memory usage and efficient circular buffer implementations. The crate supports both Rust-native usage and Python integration for strategy development and backtesting.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[average](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/average/index.html "mod nautilus_indicators::average")

Moving average type indicators.

[book](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/book/index.html "mod nautilus_indicators::book")

Order book specific indicators.

[indicator](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/indicator/index.html "mod nautilus_indicators::indicator")

A common `Indicator` trait.

[momentum](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/momentum/index.html "mod nautilus_indicators::momentum")

Momentum type indicators.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/python/index.html "mod nautilus_indicators::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[ratio](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/ratio/index.html "mod nautilus_indicators::ratio")

Ratio type indicators.

[testing](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_indicators/testing/index.html "mod nautilus_indicators::testing")

Common test related helper functions.

# nautilus_kraken - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_kraken](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_kraken/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_kraken Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_kraken/lib.rs.html#16-60)

Expand description

Kraken exchange adapter for NautilusTrader.

This adapter provides integration with the Kraken cryptocurrency exchange, supporting both Spot and Futures markets.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_kraken/index.html#features)
Features
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   REST API v2 client for market data and account operations
*   WebSocket v2 client for real-time data feeds
*   Support for Spot and Futures markets
*   Comprehensive instrument, ticker, trade, orderbook, and OHLC data
*   Prepared for execution support (orders, positions, balances)

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_kraken/index.html#api-documentation)
API Documentation
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Kraken REST API](https://docs.kraken.com/api/)
    
*   [Kraken WebSocket v2](https://docs.kraken.com/websockets-v2/)
    

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_kraken/index.html#python-bindings)
Python Bindings
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enable the `python` feature to use this adapter from Python:

    nautilus-kraken = { version = "0.52.0", features = ["python"] }

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_kraken/index.html#reexports)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# nautilus_live - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_live Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_live/lib.rs.html#16-65)

Expand description

Live system node for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-live` crate provides high-level abstractions and infrastructure for running live trading systems, including data streaming, execution management, and system lifecycle handling. It builds on top of the system kernel to provide simplified interfaces for live deployment:

*   `LiveNode` High-level abstraction for live system nodes.
*   `LiveNodeConfig` Configuration for live node deployment.
*   `AsyncRunner` for managing system real-time data flow.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `streaming`: Enables `persistence` dependency for streaming configuration.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
     (auto-enables `streaming`).
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[builder](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/builder/index.html "mod nautilus_live::builder")

Builder for constructing [`LiveNode`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/node/struct.LiveNode.html "struct nautilus_live::node::LiveNode")
 instances.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/config/index.html "mod nautilus_live::config")

Configuration types for live Nautilus system nodes.

[manager](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/manager/index.html "mod nautilus_live::manager")

Execution state manager for live trading.

[node](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/node/index.html "mod nautilus_live::node")

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/python/index.html "mod nautilus_live::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[runner](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_live/runner/index.html "mod nautilus_live::runner")

# nautilus_persistence - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_persistence](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_persistence Copy item path
==========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_persistence/lib.rs.html#16-55)

Expand description

Data persistence and storage management for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-persistence` crate provides data persistence capabilities for storing and retrieving trading data, state, and configuration.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `cloud`: Enables cloud storage backends (S3, Azure, GCP, HTTP) via `object_store`.
*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
     (auto-enables `cloud`).
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#modules)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[backend](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/backend/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::backend")

Provides an Apache Parquet backend powered by [DataFusion](https://arrow.apache.org/datafusion)
.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/config/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::config")

[parquet](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/parquet/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::parquet")

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_persistence/python/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

# nautilus_pyo3 - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_pyo3](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_pyo3 Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_pyo3/lib.rs.html#16-331)

Expand description

Python bindings aggregator crate for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-pyo3` crate collects the Python bindings generated across the NautilusTrader workspace and re-exports them through a single shared library that can be included in binary wheels.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate is primarily intended to be built for Python via [maturin](https://github.com/PyO3/maturin)
 and therefore provides a broad set of feature flags to toggle bindings and optional dependencies:

*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module (automatically enabled by `maturin`).
*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) support in dependent crates.
*   `high-precision`: Uses 128-bit value types throughout the workspace.
*   `cython-compat`: Adjusts the module name so it can be imported from Cython generated code.
*   `postgres`: Enables PostgreSQL (sqlx) back-ends in dependent crates.
*   `redis`: Enables Redis based infrastructure in dependent crates.
*   `hypersync`: Enables hypersync support (fast parallel hash maps) where available.

Functions[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#functions)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[stub\_info](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_pyo3/fn.stub_info.html "fn nautilus_pyo3::stub_info")

Generate Python type stub info for PyO3 bindings.

---

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [OKX](https://www.okx.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-okx` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models and helper utilities that wrap the official **OKX v5 API**.

The official OKX API reference can be found at [https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/](https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/)
. All public links inside this crate reference the English version.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#platform)
Platform
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#feature-flags)

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# nautilus_portfolio - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_portfolio](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_portfolio Copy item path
========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_portfolio/lib.rs.html#16-69)

Expand description

Portfolio management and risk analysis for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-portfolio` crate provides comprehensive portfolio management capabilities including real-time position tracking, performance calculations, and risk management. This includes sophisticated portfolio analytics and multi-currency support:

*   **Portfolio tracking**: Real-time portfolio state management with position and balance monitoring.
*   **Account management**: Support for cash and margin accounts across multiple venues.
*   **Performance calculations**: Real-time unrealized PnL, realized PnL, and mark-to-market valuations.
*   **Risk management**: Initial margin calculations, maintenance margin tracking, and exposure monitoring.
*   **Multi-currency support**: Currency conversion and cross-currency risk exposure analysis.
*   **Configuration options**: Flexible settings for price types, currency conversion, and portfolio behavior.

The crate handles complex portfolio scenarios including multi-venue trading, currency conversions, and sophisticated margin calculations for both live trading and backtesting environments.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#platform)
Platform
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#reexports)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use portfolio::[Portfolio](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/portfolio/struct.Portfolio.html "struct nautilus_portfolio::portfolio::Portfolio") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#modules)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/config/index.html "mod nautilus_portfolio::config")

[manager](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/manager/index.html "mod nautilus_portfolio::manager")

Provides account management functionality.

[portfolio](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_portfolio/portfolio/index.html "mod nautilus_portfolio::portfolio")

Provides a generic `Portfolio` for all environments.
