### _class_ RolloverInterestCalculator[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/accounting/#class-rolloverinterestcalculator "Direct link to class-rolloverinterestcalculator")

Bases: `object`

RolloverInterestCalculator(data: pd.DataFrame)

Provides rollover interest rate calculations.

If rate\_data\_csv\_path is empty then will default to the included short-term interest rate data csv (data since 1956).

*   **Parameters:** **data** (_str_) – The short term interest rate data.

#### calc\_overnight\_rate(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, date date)[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/accounting/#calc_overnight_rateself-instrumentid-instrument_id-date-date "Direct link to calc_overnight_rate(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, date date)")

Return the rollover interest rate between the given base currency and quote currency.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The forex instrument ID for the calculation.
    *   **date** (_date_) – The date for the overnight rate.
*   **Return type:** Decimal
*   **Raises:** **ValueError** – If instrument\_id.symbol length is not in range \[6, 7\].

# nautilus_architect_ax - Rust

[Crate nautilus\_architect\_ax](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_architect\_ax Copy item path
============================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/src/nautilus_architect_ax/lib.rs.html#16-68)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for Ax’s [AX Exchange](https://architect.exchange/)
.

[AX Exchange](https://architect.exchange/)
 is the world’s first centralized and regulated exchange for perpetual futures on traditional underlying asset classes (FX, rates, metals, energy, stock indexes). Designed for institutional and professional traders, it combines innovations from digital asset perpetual exchanges with the safety and risk management of traditional futures exchanges. Licensed under the [Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA)](https://www.bma.bm/)
.

The `nautilus-architect-ax` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models, and helper utilities that wrap the official AX Exchange API.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#platform)
Platform
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature flags
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation:

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings via [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used together with `python`).

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#documentation)
Documentation
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   API reference: [https://docs.sandbox.x.architect.co/api-reference/](https://docs.sandbox.x.architect.co/api-reference/)
    
*   Crate docs: [https://docs.rs/nautilus-architect-ax](https://docs.rs/nautilus-architect-ax)
    

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/common/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::common")

Common types, constants, and utilities shared across the AX Exchange adapter.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/config/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::config")

Configuration structures for the AX Exchange adapter.

[data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/data/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::data")

Live market data client implementation for the AX Exchange adapter.

[error](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/error/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::error")

Unified error handling for the AX Exchange adapter.

[execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/execution/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::execution")

Live execution client implementation for the AX Exchange adapter.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::factories")

Factory functions for creating AX Exchange clients and components.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/http/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::http")

HTTP REST API client for Ax.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/python/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::python")

Python bindings for the Ax adapter.

[websocket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-nightly/nautilus_architect_ax/websocket/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::websocket")

WebSocket client for Ax real-time data and execution.

# Analysis | NautilusTrader Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: nightly

On this page

The analysis subpackage groups components relating to trading performance statistics and analysis.

### _class_ CAGR[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-cagr "Direct link to class-cagr")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) for returns.

CAGR represents the mean annual growth rate of an investment over a specified period, assuming the profits were reinvested at the end of each period.

Formula: CAGR = (Ending Value / Beginning Value)^(Period/Days) - 1

For returns: CAGR = ((1 + Total Return)^(Period/Days)) - 1

#### name[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#name "Direct link to name")

Bases: `object`

### _class_ CalmarRatio[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-calmarratio "Direct link to class-calmarratio")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the Calmar Ratio for returns.

The Calmar Ratio is a function of the fund’s average compounded annual rate of return versus its maximum drawdown. The higher the Calmar ratio, the better it performed on a risk-adjusted basis during the given time frame.

Formula: Calmar Ratio = CAGR /

    |Max Drawdown|

Reference: Young, T. W. (1991). “Calmar Ratio: A Smoother Tool”. Futures, 20(1).

#### calculate\_from\_returns(raw\_returns)[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#calculate_from_returnsraw_returns-1 "Direct link to calculate_from_returns(raw_returns)")

#### name[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#name-3 "Direct link to name")

### _class_ Expectancy[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-expectancy "Direct link to class-expectancy")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the expectancy of a trading strategy based on realized PnLs.

Expectancy is defined as: (Average Win × Win Rate) + (Average Loss × Loss Rate) This metric provides insight into the expected profitability per trade and helps evaluate the overall edge of a trading strategy.

A positive expectancy indicates a profitable system over time, while a negative expectancy suggests losses.

References
==========

*   Tharp, V. K. (1998). _Trade Your Way to Financial Freedom_. McGraw-Hill.
*   Elder, A. (1993). _Trading for a Living_. John Wiley & Sons.
*   Vince, R. (1992). _The Mathematics of Money Management_. John Wiley & Sons.

    *   **cols** (_int_ _,_ _default 2_) – Number of columns in the grid.
    *   **heights** (_list_ \*\[\*_float_ _\]_ _,_ [_default_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.live.config.RoutingConfig.default)
         \*\[\*_0.50_ _,_ _0.22_ _,_ _0.16_ _,_ _0.12_ _\]_) – Relative heights for each row (must sum to 1.0 or be proportional).
    *   **vertical\_spacing** (_float_ _,_ _default 0.10_) – Vertical spacing between subplots (0.0 to 1.0).
    *   **horizontal\_spacing** (_float_ _,_ _default 0.10_) – Horizontal spacing between subplots (0.0 to 1.0).

### _class_ MaxDrawdown[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-maxdrawdown "Direct link to class-maxdrawdown")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the Maximum Drawdown for returns.

Maximum Drawdown is the maximum observed loss from a peak to a trough, before a new peak is attained. It is an indicator of downside risk over a specified time period.

Formula: Max((Peak - Trough) / Peak) for all peak-trough sequences

Return the total PnL for the portfolio.

For multi-currency portfolios, specify the currency for the result.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **currency** ([_Currency_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Currency)
         _,_ _optional_) – The currency for the result.
    *   **unrealized\_pnl** ([_Money_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Money)
         _,_ _optional_) – The unrealized PnL for the given currency.
*   **Return type:** float
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is `None` when analyzing multi-currency portfolios.
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is not contained in the tracked account balances.
    *   **ValueError** – If unrealized\_pnl is not `None` and currency is not equal to the given currency.

Return the percentage change of the total PnL for the portfolio.

For multi-currency accounts, specify the currency for the result.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **currency** ([_Currency_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Currency)

    *   **unrealized\_pnl** ([_Money_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Money)
         _,_ _optional_) – The unrealized PnL for the given currency.
*   **Return type:** float
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is `None` when analyzing multi-currency portfolios.
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is not contained in the tracked account balances.
    *   **ValueError** – If unrealized\_pnl is not `None` and currency is not equal to the given currency.

### _class_ ProfitFactor[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-profitfactor "Direct link to class-profitfactor")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the profit factor based on portfolio returns.

Profit factor is defined as the ratio of gross profits to gross losses: Sum(Positive Returns) / Abs(Sum(Negative Returns))

A profit factor greater than 1.0 indicates a profitable strategy, while a factor less than 1.0 indicates losses exceed gains.

Generally:

*   1.0-1.5: Modest profitability
*   1.5-2.0: Good profitability
*   > 2.0: Excellent profitability
    

References
==========

*   Tharp, V. K. (1998). _Trade Your Way to Financial Freedom_. McGraw-Hill.
*   Kaufman, P. J. (2013). _Trading Systems and Methods_ (5th ed.). Wiley.

### _class_ ReturnsVolatility[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-returnsvolatility "Direct link to class-returnsvolatility")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the annualized volatility (standard deviation) of portfolio returns.

Volatility is calculated as the standard deviation of returns, annualized by multiplying the daily standard deviation by the square root of the period: Standard Deviation \* sqrt(period)

Uses Bessel’s correction (ddof=1) for sample standard deviation. This provides a measure of the portfolio’s risk or uncertainty of returns.

References
==========

*   CFA Institute Level I Curriculum: Quantitative Methods
*   Hull, J. C. (2018). _Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives_ (10th ed.). Pearson.
*   Fabozzi, F. J., et al. (2002). _The Handbook of Financial Instruments_. Wiley.

#### calculate\_from\_positions(\_positions)[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#calculate_from_positions_positions-11 "Direct link to calculate_from_positions(_positions)")

### _class_ RiskReturnRatio[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-riskreturnratio "Direct link to class-riskreturnratio")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the risk-return ratio (mean/std) for portfolio returns.

This is a non-annualized ratio of mean return to standard deviation. For an annualized version, use SharpeRatio.

Downsamples high-frequency returns to daily bins before calculation for consistency with other ratio-based statistics.

#### calculate\_from\_positions(\_positions)[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#calculate_from_positions_positions-12 "Direct link to calculate_from_positions(_positions)")

### _class_ SharpeRatio[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-sharperatio "Direct link to class-sharperatio")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the Sharpe ratio for portfolio returns.

The Sharpe ratio measures risk-adjusted return and is calculated as: (Mean Return - Risk-free Rate) / Standard Deviation of Returns \* sqrt(period)

This implementation assumes a risk-free rate of 0 and annualizes the ratio using the square root of the specified period (default: 252 trading days).

References
==========

*   Sharpe, W. F. (1966). “Mutual Fund Performance”. _Journal of Business_, 39(1), 119-138.
*   Sharpe, W. F. (1994). “The Sharpe Ratio”. _Journal of Portfolio Management_, 21(1), 49-58.
*   CFA Institute Investment Foundations, 3rd Edition

### _class_ SortinoRatio[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-sortinoratio "Direct link to class-sortinoratio")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the Sortino ratio for portfolio returns.

The Sortino ratio is a variation of the Sharpe ratio that only penalizes downside volatility, making it more appropriate for strategies with asymmetric return distributions.

Formula: Mean Return / Downside Deviation \* sqrt(period)

Where downside deviation is calculated as: sqrt(sum(negative\_returns^2) / total\_observations)

Note: Uses total observations count (not just negative returns) as per Sortino’s methodology.

References
==========

*   Sortino, F. A., & van der Meer, R. (1991). “Downside Risk”. _Journal of Portfolio Management_, 17(4), 27-31.
*   Sortino, F. A., & Price, L. N. (1994). “Performance Measurement in a Downside Risk Framework”. _Journal of Investing_, 3(3), 59-64.

### _class_ TearsheetConfig[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-tearsheetconfig "Direct link to class-tearsheetconfig")

Bases: [`NautilusConfig`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.common.config.NautilusConfig)

Configuration for tearsheet generation.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **charts** (_list_ _\[_[_TearsheetChart_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#nautilus_trader.analysis.TearsheetChart)\
         _\]_ _,_ _default built-ins_) – Charts to include in the tearsheet, in order. Example: charts=\[TearsheetRunInfoChart(title=”Run Info”)\].
    *   **theme** (_str_ _,_ _default "plotly\_white"_) – Theme name for visualization styling. Built-in themes: “plotly\_white”, “plotly\_dark”, “nautilus”, “nautilus\_dark”.
    *   **layout** ([_GridLayout_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#nautilus_trader.analysis.GridLayout)
         _|_ _None_ _,_ _default None_) – Custom grid layout specification. If None, auto-calculated based on charts.
    *   **title** (_str_ _,_ _default "NautilusTrader Backtest Results"_) – Title for the tearsheet.
    *   **include\_benchmark** (_bool_ _,_ _default True_) – Whether to include benchmark comparison in visualizations. Only applies when benchmark\_returns data is provided.
    *   **benchmark\_name** (_str_ _,_ _default "Benchmark"_) – Display name for the benchmark in visualizations.
    *   **height** (_PositiveInt_ _,_ _default 1500_) – Total height of the tearsheet in pixels.
    *   **show\_logo** (_bool_ _,_ _default True_) – Whether to display NautilusTrader logo in the tearsheet.

### _class_ WinRate[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-winrate "Direct link to class-winrate")

Bases: `object`

Calculates the win rate of a trading strategy based on realized PnLs.

Win rate is the percentage of profitable trades out of total trades: Count(Trades with PnL > 0) / Total Trades

Returns a value between 0.0 and 1.0, where 1.0 represents 100% winning trades.

Note: While a high win rate is desirable, it should be considered alongside average win/loss sizes and profit factor for complete system evaluation.

References
==========

*   Standard trading performance metric across the industry
*   Tharp, V. K. (1998). _Trade Your Way to Financial Freedom_. McGraw-Hill.
*   Kaufman, P. J. (2013). _Trading Systems and Methods_ (5th ed.). Wiley.

        ) – The backtest engine with completed run.

*   **Raises:** **ImportError** – If plotly is not installed.

### create\_tearsheet\_from\_stats(stats\_pnls: dict\[str, Any\] | dict\[str, dict\[str, Any\]\], stats\_returns: dict\[str, Any\], stats\_general: dict\[str, Any\], returns: Series, output\_path: str | None = 'tearsheet.html', title: str = 'NautilusTrader Backtest Results', config=None, benchmark\_returns: Series | None = None, benchmark\_name: str = 'Benchmark', run\_info: dict\[str, Any\] | None = None, account\_info: dict\[str, Any\] | None = None, engine=None) → str | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#create_tearsheet_from_statsstats_pnls-dictstr-any--dictstr-dictstr-any-stats_returns-dictstr-any-stats_general-dictstr-any-returns-series-output_path-str--none--tearsheethtml-title-str--nautilustrader-backtest-results-confignone-benchmark_returns-series--none--none-benchmark_name-str--benchmark-run_info-dictstr-any--none--none-account_info-dictstr-any--none--none-enginenone--str--none "Direct link to create_tearsheet_from_stats(stats_pnls: dict[str, Any] | dict[str, dict[str, Any]], stats_returns: dict[str, Any], stats_general: dict[str, Any], returns: Series, output_path: str | None = 'tearsheet.html', title: str = 'NautilusTrader Backtest Results', config=None, benchmark_returns: Series | None = None, benchmark_name: str = 'Benchmark', run_info: dict[str, Any] | None = None, account_info: dict[str, Any] | None = None, engine=None) → str | None")

Generate an interactive HTML tearsheet from precomputed statistics.

This lower-level API is useful for offline analysis when you have precomputed statistics and don’t want to pass an engine.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **stats\_pnls** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_) – PnL-based statistics from analyzer.
    *   **stats\_returns** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_) – Returns-based statistics from analyzer.
    *   **stats\_general** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_) – General statistics from analyzer.
    *   **returns** (_pd.Series_) – Returns series from analyzer.
    *   **output\_path** (_str_ _,_ _optional_) – Path to save HTML tearsheet. If None, returns HTML string.
    *   **title** (_str_ _,_ _default "NautilusTrader Backtest Results"_) – Title for the tearsheet.
    *   **config** ([_TearsheetConfig_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#nautilus_trader.analysis.TearsheetConfig)
         _,_ _optional_) – Configuration for tearsheet customization. If None, uses default configuration.
    *   **benchmark\_returns** (_pd.Series_ _,_ _optional_) – Benchmark returns series for comparison. If provided, benchmark will be overlaid on visualizations.
    *   **benchmark\_name** (_str_ _,_ _default "Benchmark"_) – Display name for the benchmark.
    *   **run\_info** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Run metadata (run ID, timestamps, backtest period, event counts).
    *   **account\_info** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Account information (starting/ending balances per currency).
    *   **engine** ([_BacktestEngine_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest#nautilus_trader.backtest.engine.BacktestEngine)
         _,_ _optional_) – The backtest engine. Required for charts that need engine access (e.g., bars\_with\_fills).
*   **Returns:** HTML string if output\_path is None, otherwise None.
*   **Return type:** str or None
*   **Raises:** **ImportError** – If plotly is not installed.

### create\_yearly\_returns(returns: Series, output\_path: str | None = None, title: str = 'Yearly Returns') → Figure[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#create_yearly_returnsreturns-series-output_path-str--none--none-title-str--yearly-returns--figure "Direct link to create_yearly_returns(returns: Series, output_path: str | None = None, title: str = 'Yearly Returns') → Figure")

Create an interactive yearly returns bar chart.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **returns** (_pd.Series_) – Returns series from portfolio analyzer.
    *   **output\_path** (_str_ _,_ _optional_) – Path to save HTML plot. If None, plot is not saved.
    *   **title** (_str_ _,_ _default "Yearly Returns"_) – Plot title.
*   **Returns:** Plotly figure object.
*   **Return type:** go.Figure
*   **Raises:** **ImportError** – If plotly is not installed.

### get\_chart(name: str) → Callable[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#get_chartname-str--callable "Direct link to get_chart(name: str) → Callable")

Get registered chart function by name.

*   **Parameters:** **name** (_str_) – The chart name.
*   **Returns:** The chart function.
*   **Return type:** Callable
*   **Raises:** **KeyError** – If the chart name is not registered.

### get\_theme(name: str) → dict\[str, Any\][​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#get_themename-str--dictstr-any "Direct link to get_theme(name: str) → dict[str, Any]")

Get theme configuration by name.

*   **Parameters:** **name** (_str_) – The theme name. Built-in themes: “plotly\_white”, “plotly\_dark”, “nautilus”, “nautilus\_dark”.
*   **Returns:** Theme configuration dictionary with “template” and “colors” keys.
*   **Return type:** dict\[str, Any\]
*   **Raises:** **KeyError** – If the theme name is not registered.

### list\_charts() → list\[str\][​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#list_charts--liststr "Direct link to list_charts() → list[str]")

List all registered chart names.

*   **Returns:** List of available chart names.
*   **Return type:** list\[str\]

### list\_themes() → list\[str\][​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#list_themes--liststr "Direct link to list_themes() → list[str]")

List all registered theme names.

*   **Returns:** List of available theme names.
*   **Return type:** list\[str\]

### register\_chart(name: str, func: Callable | None = None) → Callable | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#register_chartname-str-func-callable--none--none--callable--none "Direct link to register_chart(name: str, func: Callable | None = None) → Callable | None")

Register a custom chart function for use in tearsheets.

Can be used as a decorator or called directly.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **name** (_str_) – The chart name for reference in TearsheetConfig.charts list.
        
    *   **func** (_Callable_ _,_ _optional_) – Chart function that returns a plotly Figure. Should accept (returns: pd.Series,
        
            **
        
        kwargs) as parameters. If None, returns a decorator.
        
*   **Returns:** The decorated function if used as a decorator, otherwise None.
*   **Return type:** Callable or None
*   **Raises:** **ValueError** – If name is empty or func is not callable.

### register\_theme(name: str, template: str, colors: dict\[str, str\]) → None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#register_themename-str-template-str-colors-dictstr-str--none "Direct link to register_theme(name: str, template: str, colors: dict[str, str]) → None")

Register a custom theme.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **name** (_str_) – The theme name for future reference.
    *   **template** (_str_) – Plotly template name (e.g., “plotly\_white”, “plotly\_dark”, “ggplot2”).
    *   **colors** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _str_ _\]_) – Color palette dictionary. Expected keys: “primary”, “positive”, “negative”, “neutral”, “background”, “grid”. All values should be hex color codes.
*   **Raises:** **ValueError** – If name is empty or colors dict is missing required keys.

### _class_ PortfolioAnalyzer[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#class-portfolioanalyzer-1 "Direct link to class-portfolioanalyzer-1")

Bases: `object`

Provides a portfolio performance analyzer for tracking and generating performance metrics and statistics.

#### register\_statistic(statistic: [PortfolioStatistic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#nautilus_trader.analysis.statistic.PortfolioStatistic)
) → None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#register_statisticstatistic-portfoliostatistic--none-1 "Direct link to register_statisticstatistic-portfoliostatistic--none-1")

) → None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#deregister_statisticstatistic-portfoliostatistic--none-1 "Direct link to deregister_statisticstatistic-portfoliostatistic--none-1")

#### realized\_pnls(currency: [Currency](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/objects#nautilus_trader.model.objects.Currency)
 | None = None) → Series | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#realized_pnlscurrency-currency--none--none--series--none-1 "Direct link to realized_pnlscurrency-currency--none--none--series--none-1")

Return the realized PnL for the portfolio.

For multi-currency portfolios, specify the currency for the result.

*   **Parameters:** **currency** ([_Currency_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Currency)
     _,_ _optional_) – The currency for the result.
*   **Return type:** pd.Series or `None`
*   **Raises:** **ValueError** – If currency is `None` when analyzing multi-currency portfolios.

#### total\_pnl(currency: [Currency](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/objects#nautilus_trader.model.objects.Currency)
 | None = None, unrealized\_pnl: [Money](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/objects#nautilus_trader.model.objects.Money)
 | None = None) → float[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#total_pnlcurrency-currency--none--none-unrealized_pnl-money--none--none--float-1 "Direct link to total_pnlcurrency-currency--none--none-unrealized_pnl-money--none--none--float-1")

Return the total PnL for the portfolio.

For multi-currency portfolios, specify the currency for the result.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **currency** ([_Currency_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Currency)
         _,_ _optional_) – The currency for the result.
    *   **unrealized\_pnl** ([_Money_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Money)
         _,_ _optional_) – The unrealized PnL for the given currency.
*   **Return type:** float
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is `None` when analyzing multi-currency portfolios.
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is not contained in the tracked account balances.
    *   **ValueError** – If unrealized\_pnl is not `None` and currency is not equal to the given currency.

#### total\_pnl\_percentage(currency: [Currency](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/objects#nautilus_trader.model.objects.Currency)
 | None = None, unrealized\_pnl: [Money](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/objects#nautilus_trader.model.objects.Money)
 | None = None) → float[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#total_pnl_percentagecurrency-currency--none--none-unrealized_pnl-money--none--none--float-1 "Direct link to total_pnl_percentagecurrency-currency--none--none-unrealized_pnl-money--none--none--float-1")

Return the percentage change of the total PnL for the portfolio.

For multi-currency accounts, specify the currency for the result.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **currency** ([_Currency_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Currency)
         _,_ _optional_) – The currency for the result.
    *   **unrealized\_pnl** ([_Money_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/#nautilus_trader.model.Money)
         _,_ _optional_) – The unrealized PnL for the given currency.
*   **Return type:** float
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is `None` when analyzing multi-currency portfolios.
    *   **ValueError** – If currency is not contained in the tracked account balances.
    *   **ValueError** – If unrealized\_pnl is not `None` and currency is not equal to the given currency.

*   **Return type:** dict\[str, Any\]

#### calculate\_from\_returns(returns: Series) → Any | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#calculate_from_returnsreturns-series--any--none-1 "Direct link to calculate_from_returns(returns: Series) → Any | None")

Calculate the statistic value from the given raw returns.

*   **Parameters:** **returns** (_pd.Series_) – The returns to use for the calculation.
*   **Returns:** A JSON serializable primitive.
*   **Return type:** Any or `None`

#### calculate\_from\_realized\_pnls(realized\_pnls: Series) → Any | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#calculate_from_realized_pnlsrealized_pnls-series--any--none-1 "Direct link to calculate_from_realized_pnls(realized_pnls: Series) → Any | None")

Calculate the statistic value from the given raw realized PnLs.

*   **Parameters:** **realized\_pnls** (_pd.Series_) – The raw PnLs for the calculation.

#### calculate\_from\_orders(orders: list\[[Order](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/orders#nautilus_trader.model.orders.base.Order)\
\]) → Any | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/analysis/#calculate_from_ordersorders-listorder--any--none-1 "Direct link to calculate_from_ordersorders-listorder--any--none-1")

#### calculate\_from\_positions(positions: list\[[Position](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/position#nautilus_trader.model.position.Position)\
