#### request\_aggregated\_bars(self, list bar\_types, datetime start, datetime end=None, int limit=0, ClientId client\_id=None, callback: Callable\[\[UUID4\], None\] | None = None, bool include\_external\_data=False, bool update\_subscriptions=False, bool update\_catalog=False, bool aggregate\_spread\_quotes=False, UUID4 request\_id=None, dict params=None) → [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#request_aggregated_barsself-list-bar_types-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-include_external_datafalse-bool-update_subscriptionsfalse-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4 "Direct link to request_aggregated_barsself-list-bar_types-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-include_external_datafalse-bool-update_subscriptionsfalse-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4")

Request historical aggregated Bar data for multiple bar types. The first bar is used to determine which market data type will be queried. This can either be quotes, trades or bars. If bars are queried, the first bar type needs to have a composite bar that is external (i.e. not internal/aggregated). This external bar type will be queried.

If end is `None` then will request up to the most recent data.

Once the response is received, the bar data is forwarded from the message bus to the on\_historical\_data handler. Any tick data used for aggregation is also forwarded to the on\_historical\_data handler.

If the request fails, then an error is logged.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **bar\_types** (_list_ _\[_[_BarType_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/data#nautilus_trader.model.data.BarType)\
         _\]_) – The list of bar types for the request. Composite bars can also be used and need to figure in the list after a BarType on which it depends.
    *   **start** (_datetime_) – The start datetime (UTC) of request time range. Should be left-inclusive (start <= value), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **end** (_datetime_ _,_ _optional_) – The end datetime (UTC) of request time range. If None then will be replaced with the current UTC time. Should be right-inclusive (value <= end), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **limit** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The limit on the amount of data received (quote ticks, trade ticks or bars).
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **callback** (_Callable_ _\[_ _\[_[_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)\
         _\]_ _,_ _None_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – The registered callback, to be called with the request ID when the response has completed processing.
    *   **include\_external\_data** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If True, includes the queried external data in the response.
    *   **update\_subscriptions** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If True, persists the aggregator of each bar\_type so it’s up to date for a subsequent market data subscription.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data.
    *   **aggregate\_spread\_quotes** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to activate a spread quote aggregator from leg quotes. Only applicable when requesting quote ticks for spread instruments.
    *   **request\_id** ([_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
         _,_ _optional_) – The UUID to use for the request ID. If None, a new UUID will be generated.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Returns:** The request\_id for the request.
*   **Return type:** [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
    
*   **Raises:**
    *   **TypeError** – If start is None.
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If end is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > end.
    *   **ValueError** – If bar\_types is empty.
    *   **TypeError** – If callback is not None and not of type Callable.
    *   **TypeError** – If bar\_types is empty or contains elements not of type BarType.

#### request\_bars(self, BarType bar\_type, datetime start, datetime end=None, int limit=0, ClientId client\_id=None, callback: Callable\[\[UUID4\], None\] | None = None, bool update\_catalog=False, bool join\_request=False, UUID4 request\_id=None, dict params=None) → [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)

         _\]_ _,_ _None_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – The registered callback, to be called with the request ID when the response has completed processing.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data.
    *   **aggregate\_spread\_quotes** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to activate a spread quote aggregator from leg quotes. Only applicable when the instrument\_id is a spread instrument.
    *   **join\_request** (_bool_ _,_ _optional_ _,_ _default to False_) – If a request should be joined and sorted with another one by using request\_join.
    *   **request\_id** ([_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
         _,_ _optional_) – The UUID to use for the request ID. If None, a new UUID will be generated.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Returns:** The request\_id for the request.
*   **Return type:** [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
    
*   **Raises:**
    *   **TypeError** – If start is None.
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If end is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > end.
    *   **TypeError** – If callback is not None and not of type Callable.

#### request\_trade\_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, datetime start, datetime end=None, int limit=0, ClientId client\_id=None, callback: Callable\[\[UUID4\], None\] | None = None, bool update\_catalog=False, bool join\_request=False, UUID4 request\_id=None, dict params=None) → [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#request_trade_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-join_requestfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4 "Direct link to request_trade_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-join_requestfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4")

Request historical TradeTick data.

If end is `None` then will request up to the most recent data.

Once the response is received, the trade tick data is forwarded from the message bus to the on\_historical\_data handler.

If the request fails, then an error is logged.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The tick instrument ID for the request.
    *   **start** (_datetime_) – The start datetime (UTC) of request time range. Should be left-inclusive (start <= value), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **end** (_datetime_ _,_ _optional_) – The end datetime (UTC) of request time range. If None then will be replaced with the current UTC time. Should be right-inclusive (value <= end), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **limit** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The limit on the amount of trade ticks received.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **callback** (_Callable_ _\[_ _\[_[_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)\
         _\]_ _,_ _None_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – The registered callback, to be called with the request ID when the response has completed processing.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data.
    *   **join\_request** (_bool_ _,_ _optional_ _,_ _default to False_) – If a request should be joined and sorted with another one by using request\_join.
    *   **request\_id** ([_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
         _,_ _optional_) – The UUID to use for the request ID. If None, a new UUID will be generated.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Returns:** The request\_id for the request.
*   **Return type:** [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
    
*   **Raises:**
    *   **TypeError** – If start is None.
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If end is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > end.
    *   **TypeError** – If callback is not None and not of type Callable.

#### reset(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#resetself--void-5 "Direct link to reset(self) → void")

#### resume(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#resumeself--void-3 "Direct link to resume(self) → void")

Resume the component.

While executing on\_resume() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `RESUMING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-73 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### run\_in\_executor(self, func: Callable\[..., Any\], tuple args=None, dict kwargs=None)[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#run_in_executorself-func-callable-any-tuple-argsnone-dict-kwargsnone "Direct link to run_in_executor(self, func: Callable[..., Any], tuple args=None, dict kwargs=None)")

Schedules the callable func to be executed as fn(\*args, \*\*kwargs).

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **func** (_Callable_) – The function to be executed.
    *   **args** (_positional arguments_) – The positional arguments for the call to func.
    *   **kwargs** (_arbitrary keyword arguments_) – The keyword arguments for the call to func.
*   **Returns:** The unique task identifier for the execution. This also corresponds to any future objects memory address.
*   **Return type:** [TaskId](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/common#nautilus_trader.common.executor.TaskId)
    
*   **Raises:** **TypeError** – If func is not of type Callable.

#### save(self) → dict[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#saveself--dict "Direct link to save(self) → dict")

Return the actor/strategy state dictionary to be saved.

Calls on\_save.

*   **Returns:** The strategy state to save.
*   **Return type:** dict\[str, bytes\]

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-74 "Direct link to WARNING")

Exceptions raised will be caught, logged, and reraised.

#### shutdown\_system(self, str reason=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#shutdown_systemself-str-reasonnone--void-3 "Direct link to shutdown_system(self, str reason=None) → void")

Initiate a system-wide shutdown by generating and publishing a ShutdownSystem command.

The command is handled by the system’s NautilusKernel, which will invoke either stop (synchronously) or stop\_async (asynchronously) depending on the execution context and the presence of an active event loop.

*   **Parameters:** **reason** (_str_ _,_ _optional_) – The reason for issuing the shutdown command.

#### start(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#startself--void-3 "Direct link to start(self) → void")

Start the component.

While executing on\_start() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `STARTING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-75 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### state[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#state-3 "Direct link to state")

ComponentState

Return the components current state.

*   **Return type:** ComponentState
*   **Type:** [Component.state](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/common#nautilus_trader.common.component.Component.state)
    

#### stop(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#stopself--void-3 "Direct link to stop(self) → void")

Stop the component.

While executing on\_stop() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `STOPPING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-76 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

Once subscribed, any matching mark price updates published on the message bus are forwarded to the on\_mark\_price handler.

        ) – The instrument to subscribe to.

         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.

#### subscribe\_order\_book\_at\_interval(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, BookType book\_type=BookType.L2\_MBP, int depth=0, int interval\_ms=1000, ClientId client\_id=None, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_book_at_intervalself-instrumentid-instrument_id-booktype-book_typebooktypel2_mbp-int-depth0-int-interval_ms1000-clientid-client_idnone-dict-paramsnone--void "Direct link to subscribe_order_book_at_interval(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, BookType book_type=BookType.L2_MBP, int depth=0, int interval_ms=1000, ClientId client_id=None, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to an OrderBook at a specified interval for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order book updates published on the message bus are forwarded to the on\_order\_book handler.

The DataEngine will only maintain one order book for each instrument. Because of this - the level, depth and params for the stream will be set as per the last subscription request (this will also affect all subscribers).

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The order book instrument ID to subscribe to.
    *   **book\_type** (BookType {`L1_MBP`, `L2_MBP`, `L3_MBO`}) – The order book type.
    *   **depth** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The maximum depth for the order book. A depth of 0 is maximum depth.
    *   **interval\_ms** (_int_ _,_ _default 1000_) – The order book snapshot interval (milliseconds).
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If depth is negative (< 0).
    *   **ValueError** – If interval\_ms is not positive (> 0).

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-77 "Direct link to WARNING")

Consider subscribing to order book deltas if you need intervals less than 100 milliseconds.

#### subscribe\_order\_book\_deltas(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, BookType book\_type=BookType.L2\_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client\_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3\_conversion=False, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_book_deltasself-instrumentid-instrument_id-booktype-book_typebooktypel2_mbp-int-depth0-clientid-client_idnone-bool-managedtrue-bool-pyo3_conversionfalse-dict-paramsnone--void "Direct link to subscribe_order_book_deltas(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, BookType book_type=BookType.L2_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3_conversion=False, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to the order book data stream, being a snapshot then deltas for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order book data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_order\_book\_deltas handler.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The order book instrument ID to subscribe to.
    *   **book\_type** (BookType {`L1_MBP`, `L2_MBP`, `L3_MBO`}) – The order book type.
    *   **depth** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The maximum depth for the order book. A depth of 0 is maximum depth.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **managed** (_bool_ _,_ _default True_) – If an order book should be managed by the data engine based on the subscribed feed.
    *   **pyo3\_conversion** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If received deltas should be converted to nautilus\_pyo3.OrderBookDeltas prior to being passed to the on\_order\_book\_deltas handler.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### subscribe\_order\_book\_depth(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, BookType book\_type=BookType.L2\_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client\_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3\_conversion=False, bool update\_catalog=False, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_book_depthself-instrumentid-instrument_id-booktype-book_typebooktypel2_mbp-int-depth0-clientid-client_idnone-bool-managedtrue-bool-pyo3_conversionfalse-bool-update_catalogfalse-dict-paramsnone--void "Direct link to subscribe_order_book_depth(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, BookType book_type=BookType.L2_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3_conversion=False, bool update_catalog=False, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to the order book depth stream for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order book data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_order\_book\_depth handler.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The order book instrument ID to subscribe to.
    *   **book\_type** (BookType {`L1_MBP`, `L2_MBP`, `L3_MBO`}) – The order book type.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **managed** (_bool_ _,_ _default True_) – If an order book should be managed by the data engine based on the subscribed feed.
    *   **pyo3\_conversion** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If received deltas should be converted to nautilus\_pyo3.OrderBookDepth prior to being passed to the on\_order\_book\_depth handler.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data. Only useful when downloading data during a backtest.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### subscribe\_order\_cancels(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_cancelsself-instrumentid-instrument_id--void "Direct link to subscribe_order_cancels(self, InstrumentId instrument_id) → void")

Subscribe to all order cancels for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order cancels published on the message bus are forwarded to the on\_order\_canceled handler.

*   **Parameters:** **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
    ) – The instrument to subscribe to cancels for.

Once subscribed, any matching order fills published on the message bus are forwarded to the on\_order\_filled handler.

    ) – The instrument to subscribe to fills for.

#### subscribe\_quote\_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, ClientId client\_id=None, bool update\_catalog=False, bool aggregate\_spread\_quotes=False, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_quote_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-clientid-client_idnone-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-dict-paramsnone--void "Direct link to subscribe_quote_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, ClientId client_id=None, bool update_catalog=False, bool aggregate_spread_quotes=False, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to streaming QuoteTick data for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching quote tick data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_quote\_tick handler.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The tick instrument to subscribe to.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data. Only useful when downloading data during a backtest.
    *   **aggregate\_spread\_quotes** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to activate a spread quote aggregator from leg quotes. Only applicable when the instrument\_id is a spread instrument.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### subscribe\_signal(self, str name='') → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_signalself-str-name--void "Direct link to subscribe_signal(self, str name='') → void")

Subscribe to a specific signal by name, or to all signals if no name is provided.

Once subscribed, any matching signal data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_signal handler.

*   **Parameters:** **name** (_str_ _,_ _optional_) – The name of the signal to subscribe to. If not provided or an empty string is passed, the subscription will include all signals. The signal name is case-insensitive and will be capitalized (e.g., ‘example’ becomes ‘SignalExample\*’).

### _class_ SimulationModule[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#class-simulationmodule "Direct link to class-simulationmodule")

Bases: [`Actor`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/common#nautilus_trader.common.actor.Actor)

SimulationModule(config: SimulationModuleConfig)

The base class for all simulation modules.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-78 "Direct link to WARNING")

This class should not be used directly, but through a concrete subclass.

#### active\_task\_ids(self) → list[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#active_task_idsself--list-1 "Direct link to active_task_ids(self) → list")

Return the active task identifiers.

*   **Return type:** list\[[TaskId](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/common#nautilus_trader.common.executor.TaskId)\
    \]

) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#cancel_taskself-task_id-taskid--void-1 "Direct link to cancel_taskself-task_id-taskid--void-1")

#### degrade(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#degradeself--void-4 "Direct link to degrade(self) → void")

Degrade the component.

While executing on\_degrade() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `DEGRADING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-79 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### deregister\_warning\_event(self, type event) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#deregister_warning_eventself-type-event--void-1 "Direct link to deregister_warning_event(self, type event) → void")

Deregister the given event type from warning log levels.

*   **Parameters:** **event** ([_type_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#nautilus_trader.backtest.modules.SimulationModule.type)
    ) – The event class to deregister.

#### dispose(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#disposeself--void-4 "Direct link to dispose(self) → void")

Dispose of the component.

While executing on\_dispose() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `DISPOSING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-80 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### exchange[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#exchange-1 "Direct link to exchange")

#### fault(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#faultself--void-4 "Direct link to fault(self) → void")

Fault the component.

Calling this method multiple times has the same effect as calling it once (it is idempotent). Once called, it cannot be reversed, and no other methods should be called on this instance.

While executing on\_fault() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `FAULTING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-81 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### publish\_signal(self, str name, value, uint64\_t ts\_event=0) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#publish_signalself-str-name-value-uint64_t-ts_event0--void-1 "Direct link to publish_signal(self, str name, value, uint64_t ts_event=0) → void")

Publish the given value as a signal to the message bus.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **name** (_str_) – The name of the signal being published. The signal name will be converted to title case, with each word capitalized (e.g., ‘example’ becomes ‘SignalExample’).
    *   **value** (_object_) – The signal data to publish.
    *   **ts\_event** (_uint64\_t_ _,_ _optional_) – UNIX timestamp (nanoseconds) when the signal event occurred. If `None` then will timestamp current time.

#### queue\_for\_executor(self, func: Callable\[..., Any\], tuple args=None, dict kwargs=None)[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#queue_for_executorself-func-callable-any-tuple-argsnone-dict-kwargsnone-1 "Direct link to queue_for_executor(self, func: Callable[..., Any], tuple args=None, dict kwargs=None)")

Queues the callable func to be executed as fn(\*args, \*\*kwargs) sequentially.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **func** (_Callable_) – The function to be executed.
    *   **args** (_positional arguments_) – The positional arguments for the call to func.
    *   **kwargs** (_arbitrary keyword arguments_) – The keyword arguments for the call to func.
*   **Raises:** **TypeError** – If func is not of type Callable.

#### request\_aggregated\_bars(self, list bar\_types, datetime start, datetime end=None, int limit=0, ClientId client\_id=None, callback: Callable\[\[UUID4\], None\] | None = None, bool include\_external\_data=False, bool update\_subscriptions=False, bool update\_catalog=False, bool aggregate\_spread\_quotes=False, UUID4 request\_id=None, dict params=None) → [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#request_aggregated_barsself-list-bar_types-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-include_external_datafalse-bool-update_subscriptionsfalse-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4-1 "Direct link to request_aggregated_barsself-list-bar_types-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-include_external_datafalse-bool-update_subscriptionsfalse-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4-1")

Request historical aggregated Bar data for multiple bar types. The first bar is used to determine which market data type will be queried. This can either be quotes, trades or bars. If bars are queried, the first bar type needs to have a composite bar that is external (i.e. not internal/aggregated). This external bar type will be queried.

If end is `None` then will request up to the most recent data.

Once the response is received, the bar data is forwarded from the message bus to the on\_historical\_data handler. Any tick data used for aggregation is also forwarded to the on\_historical\_data handler.

If the request fails, then an error is logged.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **bar\_types** (_list_ _\[_[_BarType_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/data#nautilus_trader.model.data.BarType)\
         _\]_) – The list of bar types for the request. Composite bars can also be used and need to figure in the list after a BarType on which it depends.
    *   **start** (_datetime_) – The start datetime (UTC) of request time range. Should be left-inclusive (start <= value), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **end** (_datetime_ _,_ _optional_) – The end datetime (UTC) of request time range. If None then will be replaced with the current UTC time. Should be right-inclusive (value <= end), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **limit** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The limit on the amount of data received (quote ticks, trade ticks or bars).
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **callback** (_Callable_ _\[_ _\[_[_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)\
         _\]_ _,_ _None_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – The registered callback, to be called with the request ID when the response has completed processing.
    *   **include\_external\_data** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If True, includes the queried external data in the response.
    *   **update\_subscriptions** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If True, persists the aggregator of each bar\_type so it’s up to date for a subsequent market data subscription.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data.
    *   **aggregate\_spread\_quotes** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to activate a spread quote aggregator from leg quotes. Only applicable when requesting quote ticks for spread instruments.
    *   **request\_id** ([_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
         _,_ _optional_) – The UUID to use for the request ID. If None, a new UUID will be generated.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Returns:** The request\_id for the request.
*   **Return type:** [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
    
*   **Raises:**
    *   **TypeError** – If start is None.
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If end is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > end.
    *   **ValueError** – If bar\_types is empty.
    *   **TypeError** – If callback is not None and not of type Callable.
    *   **TypeError** – If bar\_types is empty or contains elements not of type BarType.

*   **Return type:** [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
    
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If the instrument\_id is None.
    *   **TypeError** – If callback is not None and not of type Callable.

#### request\_quote\_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, datetime start, datetime end=None, int limit=0, ClientId client\_id=None, callback: Callable\[\[UUID4\], None\] | None = None, bool update\_catalog=False, bool aggregate\_spread\_quotes=False, bool join\_request=False, UUID4 request\_id=None, dict params=None) → [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#request_quote_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-bool-join_requestfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4-1 "Direct link to request_quote_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-bool-join_requestfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4-1")

Request historical QuoteTick data.

If end is `None` then will request up to the most recent data.

Once the response is received, the quote tick data is forwarded from the message bus to the on\_historical\_data handler.

If the request fails, then an error is logged.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The tick instrument ID for the request.
    *   **start** (_datetime_) – The start datetime (UTC) of request time range. Should be left-inclusive (start <= value), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **end** (_datetime_ _,_ _optional_) – The end datetime (UTC) of request time range. If None then will be replaced with the current UTC time. Should be right-inclusive (value <= end), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **limit** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The limit on the amount of quote ticks received.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **callback** (_Callable_ _\[_ _\[_[_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)\
         _\]_ _,_ _None_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – The registered callback, to be called with the request ID when the response has completed processing.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data.
    *   **aggregate\_spread\_quotes** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to activate a spread quote aggregator from leg quotes. Only applicable when the instrument\_id is a spread instrument.
    *   **join\_request** (_bool_ _,_ _optional_ _,_ _default to False_) – If a request should be joined and sorted with another one by using request\_join.
    *   **request\_id** ([_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
         _,_ _optional_) – The UUID to use for the request ID. If None, a new UUID will be generated.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Returns:** The request\_id for the request.
*   **Return type:** [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
    
*   **Raises:**
    *   **TypeError** – If start is None.
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If end is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > end.
    *   **TypeError** – If callback is not None and not of type Callable.

#### request\_trade\_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, datetime start, datetime end=None, int limit=0, ClientId client\_id=None, callback: Callable\[\[UUID4\], None\] | None = None, bool update\_catalog=False, bool join\_request=False, UUID4 request\_id=None, dict params=None) → [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#request_trade_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-join_requestfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4-1 "Direct link to request_trade_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-datetime-start-datetime-endnone-int-limit0-clientid-client_idnone-callback-callableuuid4-none--none--none-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-join_requestfalse-uuid4-request_idnone-dict-paramsnone--uuid4-1")

Request historical TradeTick data.

If end is `None` then will request up to the most recent data.

Once the response is received, the trade tick data is forwarded from the message bus to the on\_historical\_data handler.

If the request fails, then an error is logged.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The tick instrument ID for the request.
    *   **start** (_datetime_) – The start datetime (UTC) of request time range. Should be left-inclusive (start <= value), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **end** (_datetime_ _,_ _optional_) – The end datetime (UTC) of request time range. If None then will be replaced with the current UTC time. Should be right-inclusive (value <= end), but inclusiveness is not currently guaranteed.
    *   **limit** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The limit on the amount of trade ticks received.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **callback** (_Callable_ _\[_ _\[_[_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)\
         _\]_ _,_ _None_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – The registered callback, to be called with the request ID when the response has completed processing.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data.
    *   **join\_request** (_bool_ _,_ _optional_ _,_ _default to False_) – If a request should be joined and sorted with another one by using request\_join.
    *   **request\_id** ([_UUID4_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
         _,_ _optional_) – The UUID to use for the request ID. If None, a new UUID will be generated.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Returns:** The request\_id for the request.
*   **Return type:** [UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/core#nautilus_trader.core.UUID4)
    
*   **Raises:**
    *   **TypeError** – If start is None.
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If end is > current timestamp (now).
    *   **ValueError** – If start is > end.
    *   **TypeError** – If callback is not None and not of type Callable.

#### reset(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#resetself--void-6 "Direct link to reset(self) → void")

Abstract method (implement in subclass).

#### resume(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#resumeself--void-4 "Direct link to resume(self) → void")

Resume the component.

While executing on\_resume() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `RESUMING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-127 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### run\_in\_executor(self, func: Callable\[..., Any\], tuple args=None, dict kwargs=None)[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#run_in_executorself-func-callable-any-tuple-argsnone-dict-kwargsnone-1 "Direct link to run_in_executor(self, func: Callable[..., Any], tuple args=None, dict kwargs=None)")

Schedules the callable func to be executed as fn(\*args, \*\*kwargs).

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **func** (_Callable_) – The function to be executed.
    *   **args** (_positional arguments_) – The positional arguments for the call to func.
    *   **kwargs** (_arbitrary keyword arguments_) – The keyword arguments for the call to func.
*   **Returns:** The unique task identifier for the execution. This also corresponds to any future objects memory address.
*   **Return type:** [TaskId](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/common#nautilus_trader.common.executor.TaskId)
    
*   **Raises:** **TypeError** – If func is not of type Callable.

#### save(self) → dict[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#saveself--dict-1 "Direct link to save(self) → dict")

Return the actor/strategy state dictionary to be saved.

Calls on\_save.

*   **Returns:** The strategy state to save.
*   **Return type:** dict\[str, bytes\]

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-128 "Direct link to WARNING")

Exceptions raised will be caught, logged, and reraised.

#### shutdown\_system(self, str reason=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#shutdown_systemself-str-reasonnone--void-4 "Direct link to shutdown_system(self, str reason=None) → void")

Initiate a system-wide shutdown by generating and publishing a ShutdownSystem command.

The command is handled by the system’s NautilusKernel, which will invoke either stop (synchronously) or stop\_async (asynchronously) depending on the execution context and the presence of an active event loop.

*   **Parameters:** **reason** (_str_ _,_ _optional_) – The reason for issuing the shutdown command.

#### start(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#startself--void-4 "Direct link to start(self) → void")

Start the component.

While executing on\_start() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `STARTING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-129 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### state[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#state-4 "Direct link to state")

ComponentState

Return the components current state.

*   **Return type:** ComponentState
*   **Type:** [Component.state](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/common#nautilus_trader.common.component.Component.state)
    

#### stop(self) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#stopself--void-4 "Direct link to stop(self) → void")

Stop the component.

While executing on\_stop() any exception will be logged and reraised, then the component will remain in a `STOPPING` state.

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-130 "Direct link to WARNING")

Do not override.

If the component is not in a valid state from which to execute this method, then the component state will not change, and an error will be logged.

#### subscribe\_bars(self, BarType bar\_type, ClientId client\_id=None, bool update\_catalog=False, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_barsself-bartype-bar_type-clientid-client_idnone-bool-update_catalogfalse-dict-paramsnone--void-1 "Direct link to subscribe_bars(self, BarType bar_type, ClientId client_id=None, bool update_catalog=False, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to streaming Bar data for the given bar type.

Once subscribed, any matching bar data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_bar handler.

    *   **bar\_type** ([_BarType_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/data#nautilus_trader.model.data.BarType)

         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data. Only useful when downloading data during a backtest.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### subscribe\_order\_book\_at\_interval(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, BookType book\_type=BookType.L2\_MBP, int depth=0, int interval\_ms=1000, ClientId client\_id=None, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_book_at_intervalself-instrumentid-instrument_id-booktype-book_typebooktypel2_mbp-int-depth0-int-interval_ms1000-clientid-client_idnone-dict-paramsnone--void-1 "Direct link to subscribe_order_book_at_interval(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, BookType book_type=BookType.L2_MBP, int depth=0, int interval_ms=1000, ClientId client_id=None, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to an OrderBook at a specified interval for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order book updates published on the message bus are forwarded to the on\_order\_book handler.

The DataEngine will only maintain one order book for each instrument. Because of this - the level, depth and params for the stream will be set as per the last subscription request (this will also affect all subscribers).

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The order book instrument ID to subscribe to.
    *   **book\_type** (BookType {`L1_MBP`, `L2_MBP`, `L3_MBO`}) – The order book type.
    *   **depth** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The maximum depth for the order book. A depth of 0 is maximum depth.
    *   **interval\_ms** (_int_ _,_ _default 1000_) – The order book snapshot interval (milliseconds).
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If depth is negative (< 0).
    *   **ValueError** – If interval\_ms is not positive (> 0).

#### WARNING[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#warning-131 "Direct link to WARNING")

Consider subscribing to order book deltas if you need intervals less than 100 milliseconds.

#### subscribe\_order\_book\_deltas(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, BookType book\_type=BookType.L2\_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client\_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3\_conversion=False, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_book_deltasself-instrumentid-instrument_id-booktype-book_typebooktypel2_mbp-int-depth0-clientid-client_idnone-bool-managedtrue-bool-pyo3_conversionfalse-dict-paramsnone--void-1 "Direct link to subscribe_order_book_deltas(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, BookType book_type=BookType.L2_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3_conversion=False, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to the order book data stream, being a snapshot then deltas for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order book data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_order\_book\_deltas handler.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The order book instrument ID to subscribe to.
    *   **book\_type** (BookType {`L1_MBP`, `L2_MBP`, `L3_MBO`}) – The order book type.
    *   **depth** (_int_ _,_ _optional_) – The maximum depth for the order book. A depth of 0 is maximum depth.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **managed** (_bool_ _,_ _default True_) – If an order book should be managed by the data engine based on the subscribed feed.
    *   **pyo3\_conversion** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If received deltas should be converted to nautilus\_pyo3.OrderBookDeltas prior to being passed to the on\_order\_book\_deltas handler.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### subscribe\_order\_book\_depth(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, BookType book\_type=BookType.L2\_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client\_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3\_conversion=False, bool update\_catalog=False, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_book_depthself-instrumentid-instrument_id-booktype-book_typebooktypel2_mbp-int-depth0-clientid-client_idnone-bool-managedtrue-bool-pyo3_conversionfalse-bool-update_catalogfalse-dict-paramsnone--void-1 "Direct link to subscribe_order_book_depth(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, BookType book_type=BookType.L2_MBP, int depth=0, ClientId client_id=None, bool managed=True, bool pyo3_conversion=False, bool update_catalog=False, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to the order book depth stream for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order book data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_order\_book\_depth handler.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The order book instrument ID to subscribe to.
    *   **book\_type** (BookType {`L1_MBP`, `L2_MBP`, `L3_MBO`}) – The order book type.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **managed** (_bool_ _,_ _default True_) – If an order book should be managed by the data engine based on the subscribed feed.
    *   **pyo3\_conversion** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – If received deltas should be converted to nautilus\_pyo3.OrderBookDepth prior to being passed to the on\_order\_book\_depth handler.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data. Only useful when downloading data during a backtest.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### subscribe\_order\_cancels(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_order_cancelsself-instrumentid-instrument_id--void-1 "Direct link to subscribe_order_cancels(self, InstrumentId instrument_id) → void")

Subscribe to all order cancels for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching order cancels published on the message bus are forwarded to the on\_order\_canceled handler.

    ) – The instrument to subscribe to cancels for.

Once subscribed, any matching order fills published on the message bus are forwarded to the on\_order\_filled handler.

#### subscribe\_quote\_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, ClientId client\_id=None, bool update\_catalog=False, bool aggregate\_spread\_quotes=False, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_quote_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-clientid-client_idnone-bool-update_catalogfalse-bool-aggregate_spread_quotesfalse-dict-paramsnone--void-1 "Direct link to subscribe_quote_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, ClientId client_id=None, bool update_catalog=False, bool aggregate_spread_quotes=False, dict params=None) → void")

Subscribe to streaming QuoteTick data for the given instrument ID.

Once subscribed, any matching quote tick data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_quote\_tick handler.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The tick instrument to subscribe to.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **update\_catalog** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to update a catalog with the received data. Only useful when downloading data during a backtest.
    *   **aggregate\_spread\_quotes** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to activate a spread quote aggregator from leg quotes. Only applicable when the instrument\_id is a spread instrument.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### subscribe\_signal(self, str name='') → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#subscribe_signalself-str-name--void-1 "Direct link to subscribe_signal(self, str name='') → void")

Subscribe to a specific signal by name, or to all signals if no name is provided.

Once subscribed, any matching signal data published on the message bus is forwarded to the on\_signal handler.

*   **Parameters:** **name** (_str_ _,_ _optional_) – The name of the signal to subscribe to. If not provided or an empty string is passed, the subscription will include all signals. The signal name is case-insensitive and will be capitalized (e.g., ‘example’ becomes ‘SignalExample\*’).

    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The tick instrument to unsubscribe from.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **aggregate\_spread\_quotes** (_bool_ _,_ _default False_) – Whether to unsubscribe from a spread quote aggregator. Only applicable when the instrument\_id is a spread instrument.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### unsubscribe\_trade\_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument\_id, ClientId client\_id=None, dict params=None) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#unsubscribe_trade_ticksself-instrumentid-instrument_id-clientid-client_idnone-dict-paramsnone--void-1 "Direct link to unsubscribe_trade_ticks(self, InstrumentId instrument_id, ClientId client_id=None, dict params=None) → void")

Unsubscribe from streaming TradeTick data for the given instrument ID.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **instrument\_id** ([_InstrumentId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.InstrumentId)
        ) – The tick instrument ID to unsubscribe from.
    *   **client\_id** ([_ClientId_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/identifiers#nautilus_trader.model.identifiers.ClientId)
         _,_ _optional_) – The specific client ID for the command. If `None` then will be inferred from the venue in the instrument ID.
    *   **params** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ _Any_ _\]_ _,_ _optional_) – Additional parameters potentially used by a specific client.

#### update\_synthetic(self, SyntheticInstrument synthetic) → void[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#update_syntheticself-syntheticinstrument-synthetic--void-1 "Direct link to update_synthetic(self, SyntheticInstrument synthetic) → void")

Update the synthetic instrument in the cache.

*   **Parameters:** **synthetic** ([_SyntheticInstrument_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/model/instruments#nautilus_trader.model.instruments.SyntheticInstrument)
    ) – The synthetic instrument to update in the cache.
*   **Raises:** **KeyError** – If synthetic does not already exist in the cache.

### _class_ BacktestNode[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#class-backtestnode "Direct link to class-backtestnode")

Bases: `object`

Provides a node for orchestrating groups of backtest runs.

*   **Parameters:** **configs** (_list_ _\[_[_BacktestRunConfig_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.backtest.config.BacktestRunConfig)\
     _\]_) – The backtest run configurations.
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If configs is `None` or empty.
    *   **ValueError** – If configs contains a type other than BacktestRunConfig.

#### _property_ configs _: list\[[BacktestRunConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.backtest.config.BacktestRunConfig)\
\]_[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#property-configs--listbacktestrunconfig "Direct link to property-configs--listbacktestrunconfig")

Return the loaded backtest run configs for the node.

*   **Return type:** list\[[BacktestRunConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.backtest.config.BacktestRunConfig)\
    \]

 | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_log_guard--logguard--logguard--none "Direct link to get_log_guard--logguard--logguard--none")

May return `None` if no internal engines are initialized yet.

     | None

 | None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_enginerun_config_id-str--backtestengine--none "Direct link to get_enginerun_config_id-str--backtestengine--none")

Return the backtest engine associated with the given run config ID (if found).

*   **Parameters:** **run\_config\_id** (_str_) – The run configuration ID for the created engine.
*   **Return type:** BacktestEngine or `None`

\][​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_engines--listbacktestengine "Direct link to get_engines--listbacktestengine")

    \]

#### add\_data\_client\_factory(name: str, factory: [type](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/accounting#nautilus_trader.accounting.accounts.cash.CashAccount.type)
\[LiveDataClientFactory\]) → None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#add_data_client_factoryname-str-factory-typelivedataclientfactory--none "Direct link to add_data_client_factoryname-str-factory-typelivedataclientfactory--none")

Add the given data client factory to the node.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **name** (_str_) – The name of the client factory.
    *   **factory** ([_type_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/accounting#nautilus_trader.accounting.accounts.cash.CashAccount.type)
         \*\[\*_LiveDataClientFactory_ _\]_) – The factory class to add.
*   **Raises:**
    *   **ValueError** – If name is not a valid string.
    *   **KeyError** – If name has already been added.

#### build() → None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#build--none "Direct link to build() → None")

Can be optionally run before a backtest to build backtest engines for all configured backtest runs.

This can be useful to subscribe to a topic before running a backtest to collect any type of information.

#### setup\_download\_engine(catalog\_config: [DataCatalogConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.persistence.config.DataCatalogConfig)
, data\_clients: dict\[str, [type](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/accounting#nautilus_trader.accounting.accounts.cash.CashAccount.type)\
\[[LiveDataClientConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.live.config.LiveDataClientConfig)\
\]\]) → None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#setup_download_enginecatalog_config-datacatalogconfig-data_clients-dictstr-typelivedataclientconfig--none "Direct link to setup_download_enginecatalog_config-datacatalogconfig-data_clients-dictstr-typelivedataclientconfig--none")

Set up a backtest engine for downloading data.

Creates a dedicated backtest engine with an actor for data downloading purposes.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **catalog\_config** ([_DataCatalogConfig_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.persistence.config.DataCatalogConfig)
        ) – The configuration for the data catalog.
    *   **data\_clients** (_dict_ \*\[\*_str_ _,_ [_LiveDataClientConfig_](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/config#nautilus_trader.live.config.LiveDataClientConfig)\
         _\]_) – The data client configurations.

#### download\_data(request\_function: str, \*\*kwargs) → None[​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#download_datarequest_function-str-kwargs--none "Direct link to download_data(request_function: str, **kwargs) → None")

Download data using the specified request function.

*   **Parameters:**
    *   **request\_function** (_str_) – The name of the request function to use. Must be one of: “request\_instrument”, “request\_data”, “request\_bars”, “request\_quote\_ticks”, or “request\_trade\_ticks”.
    *   **\*\*kwargs** – Additional keyword arguments to pass to the request function.

#### run() → list\[[BacktestResult](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#nautilus_trader.backtest.results.BacktestResult)\
\][​](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#run--listbacktestresult "Direct link to run--listbacktestresult")

Run the backtest node which will synchronously execute the list of loaded backtest run configs.

    \]

*   [get\_starting\_balances(config: BacktestVenueConfig) → list\[Money\]](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_starting_balancesconfig-backtestvenueconfig--listmoney)
    
*   [get\_base\_currency(config: BacktestVenueConfig) → Currency | None](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_base_currencyconfig-backtestvenueconfig--currency--none)
    
*   [get\_leverages(config: BacktestVenueConfig) → dict\[InstrumentId, Decimal\]](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_leveragesconfig-backtestvenueconfig--dictinstrumentid-decimal)
    
*   [get\_price\_protection\_points(config: BacktestVenueConfig) → int | None](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_price_protection_pointsconfig-backtestvenueconfig--int--none)
    
*   [get\_fill\_model(config: BacktestVenueConfig) → FillModel | None](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_fill_modelconfig-backtestvenueconfig--fillmodel--none)
    
*   [get\_latency\_model(config: BacktestVenueConfig) → LatencyModel | None](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_latency_modelconfig-backtestvenueconfig--latencymodel--none)
    
*   [get\_fee\_model(config: BacktestVenueConfig) → FeeModel | None](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_fee_modelconfig-backtestvenueconfig--feemodel--none)
    
*   [get\_margin\_model(config: BacktestVenueConfig)](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/api_reference/backtest/#get_margin_modelconfig-backtestvenueconfig)

# nautilus_binance::common::fixed - Rust

[Module fixed](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/fixed/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_binance](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html)
::[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/index.html)

Module fixed Copy item path
===========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_binance/common/fixed.rs.html#16-176)

Expand description

Fixed-point conversion utilities for Binance SBE mantissa/exponent values.

Binance SBE responses encode numeric values as mantissa + exponent pairs. These utilities convert directly to Nautilus fixed-point types using pure integer arithmetic, avoiding floating-point precision loss.

Functions[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/fixed/index.html#functions)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[mantissa\_to\_f64](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/fixed/fn.mantissa_to_f64.html "fn nautilus_binance::common::fixed::mantissa_to_f64")

Converts a mantissa/exponent pair to f64 for display/debugging only.

[mantissa\_to\_price](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/fixed/fn.mantissa_to_price.html "fn nautilus_binance::common::fixed::mantissa_to_price")

Converts a mantissa/exponent pair to a Nautilus \[`Price`\].

[mantissa\_to\_quantity](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/fixed/fn.mantissa_to_quantity.html "fn nautilus_binance::common::fixed::mantissa_to_quantity")

Converts a mantissa/exponent pair to a Nautilus \[`Quantity`\].

---

# nautilus_binance::common::credential - Rust

[Module credential](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_binance](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html)
::[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/index.html)

Module credential Copy item path
================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_binance/common/credential.rs.html#16-202)

Expand description

Binance API credential handling and request signing.

This module provides two types of credentials:

*   [`Credential`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/struct.Credential.html "struct nautilus_binance::common::credential::Credential")
    : HMAC SHA256 signing for REST API and standard WebSocket
*   [`Ed25519Credential`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/struct.Ed25519Credential.html "struct nautilus_binance::common::credential::Ed25519Credential")
    : Ed25519 signing for SBE market data streams

Structs[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/index.html#structs)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Credential](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/struct.Credential.html "struct nautilus_binance::common::credential::Credential")

Binance API credentials for signing requests (HMAC SHA256).

[Ed25519Credential](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/struct.Ed25519Credential.html "struct nautilus_binance::common::credential::Ed25519Credential")

Binance Ed25519 credentials for SBE market data streams.

Enums[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/index.html#enums)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Ed25519CredentialError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/common/credential/enum.Ed25519CredentialError.html "enum nautilus_binance::common::credential::Ed25519CredentialError")

# nautilus_binance::spot - Rust

[Module spot](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/index.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_binance](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html)

Module spot Copy item path
==========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_binance/spot/mod.rs.html#16-49)

Expand description

Binance Spot market adapter with full SBE (Simple Binary Encoding) support.

This module provides high-performance market data and execution clients for Binance Spot markets using SBE encoding for both REST API and WebSocket streams.

### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/index.html#features)
Features

*   **SBE REST API**: All REST responses decoded from SBE format
*   **SBE WebSocket Streams**: Market data streams with microsecond timestamps
*   **Ed25519 Authentication**: Required for SBE market data streams

### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/index.html#architecture)
Architecture

    spot/
    ├── http/           # REST API client (SBE encoded)
    │   ├── client.rs   # BinanceSpotHttpClient
    │   ├── models.rs   # Response types
    │   └── query.rs    # Query parameter builders
    └── websocket/      # WebSocket client (SBE encoded)
        ├── client.rs   # BinanceSpotWebSocketClient
        ├── handler.rs  # Message parsing and routing
        ├── messages.rs # Internal message types
        └── streams.rs  # Stream subscription management

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/index.html#reexports)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use enums::[BinanceCancelReplaceMode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/enums/enum.BinanceCancelReplaceMode.html "enum nautilus_binance::spot::enums::BinanceCancelReplaceMode") ;`

`pub use enums::[BinanceOrderResponseType](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/enums/enum.BinanceOrderResponseType.html "enum nautilus_binance::spot::enums::BinanceOrderResponseType") ;`

`pub use enums::[BinanceSpotOrderType](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/enums/enum.BinanceSpotOrderType.html "enum nautilus_binance::spot::enums::BinanceSpotOrderType") ;`

`pub use http::client::[BinanceSpotHttpClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/http/client/struct.BinanceSpotHttpClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::spot::http::client::BinanceSpotHttpClient") ;`

`pub use websocket::client::[BinanceSpotWebSocketClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/websocket/client/struct.BinanceSpotWebSocketClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::spot::websocket::client::BinanceSpotWebSocketClient") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/index.html#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[enums](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/enums/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::spot::enums")

Binance Spot-specific enumerations.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/http/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::spot::http")

Binance Spot HTTP client with SBE encoding support.

[websocket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/websocket/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::spot::websocket")

Binance Spot WebSocket client with SBE encoding support.

# nautilus_binance::futures - Rust

[Module futures](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_binance](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html)

Module futures Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/src/nautilus_binance/futures/mod.rs.html#16-42)

Expand description

Binance Futures adapter components.

This module provides HTTP and WebSocket clients for Binance Futures:

*   **USD-M Futures** (`fapi.binance.com`) - USDT-margined perpetual contracts
*   **COIN-M Futures** (`dapi.binance.com`) - Coin-margined perpetual contracts

### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#websocket-streams)
WebSocket Streams

Unlike Spot which uses SBE binary encoding, Futures uses standard JSON WebSocket streams:

*   `<symbol>@trade` - Real-time trade data
*   `<symbol>@depth` - Order book updates (diff)
*   `<symbol>@depth@100ms` - Order book updates (100ms frequency)
*   `<symbol>@markPrice` - Mark price updates
*   `<symbol>@kline_<interval>` - Kline/candlestick updates

### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#authentication)
Authentication

*   Public streams: No authentication required
*   User data streams: Requires listen key (obtained via REST API)

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#reexports)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use http::client::[BinanceFuturesHttpClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/http/client/struct.BinanceFuturesHttpClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::futures::http::client::BinanceFuturesHttpClient") ;`

`pub use websocket::client::[BinanceFuturesWebSocketClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/core-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/websocket/client/struct.BinanceFuturesWebSocketClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::futures::websocket::client::BinanceFuturesWebSocketClient") ;`
